en_body:
The bell rang, classes ended. Class 11A3 clamored like a marketplace broken loose. Seventy-two schoolgirls, seventy-two subjects, seventy-two voices laughing and talking, softly, loudly, making dates, calling one another. Thúy sat at the fifth desk near the back of the room. Vi sat silently listening to Phượng talk about her "newly-met young man."
--Thành is so gentle, you know! He passed the baccalaureate with an Average rank, yet though he loves learning, his family is poor, so he had to enter the army. He is tall and terribly handsome! Today he is coming to meet me. Don't tease me when you meet Thành, hear me! That Thúy is so very mischievous, I dare not tell her. Once she gets started...I can only sit there and cry from shame!
--How do you know I won't tease you?
--Please, all right? Don't tease me; I tell you because I trust you, don't I?
--I am only saying. I am easy to please, am I not, Phượng? Just one packet of Nguyễn Bỉnh Khiêm licorice ô-mai every day, agreed?
 Vi slipped an arm around Phượng's shoulders. The girl smiled, her eyes looking out upon the broad sunlit schoolyard beyond the classroom window. That faraway gaze seemed already leaving behind the innocent sky on this side. Her smiling lips were truly intoxicated, as though dazed by the wine of growing up, yet Vi felt that she was sad. Vi softly held Phượng's hand, wondering how that strange world Phượng was entering had come into her...surely it must hold both Happiness and bewilderment.

--Hey, why all this hand-holding and such tenderness? You want to make me jealous, is that it?
 Thúy was always the most talkative and carefree girl among them. Phượng stood and reached to fondle Thúy's chin:
--You are like a baby, Thúy!
--What if I am? Babies get to demand things, don't you know?
 The three girlfriends held hands and threaded their way into line. The corridor was narrow; lined four abreast, there was no room to move. A whole realm of girls laughed and talked, merry as a festival. The afternoon sun lay dryly golden upon the playground. The Yellow Cườm-Thảo was setting fruit, its buds still green as leaves. Looking from outside, no one could know how many joys and sorrows were newly budding among those girls in white áo-dài, their dreamlike panels flowing. Vi stood silent, and when the river of white-clad girls began to move, she let herself drift within that jubilant white stream. Suddenly she looked at the rustling rows of trees below, sunlight pouring upon the leaves, light flickering. And when she had just passed through the school gate, so that now she stood beneath a tree and looked upward, a region of drifting cloud was covering the clear frame of sky. Amid friends laughing and talking noisily, Phượng had slipped away to wait for Thành somewhere, at the end of some road. Thúy was weaving through the crowd to buy two cups of white-bean sweet soup with coconut milk. Vi was astonished to feel her heart weightless and empty. She heard herself changing, as though the earth had completed one revolution upon its own axis and was beginning another. And every revolution is a new day unlike any other; every new day holds changes, holds beginnings bringing growth.
 Vi was neither sad nor happy. She watched Thúy carrying the two cups of sweet soup toward her in delight, smiling as carefree as sunlight suddenly bright when the cloud has flown away. The two girls stood eating their sweet soup; across the street, the young man with the wonderfully radiant smile from the other day was lingering, looking at Vi...smiling beautifully.
note: The schoolgirls' white áo-dài and the Nguyễn Bỉnh Khiêm ô-mai evoke the textures of pre-1975 Vietnamese student life.
